titleStmt.title: SII 9.92: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 92. (A.R. No. 200 of 1913.) ON A MUTILATED STONE LYING NEAR THE SAME TEMPLE.

summary.p: This is a little damaged and is of the same date as the above, i.e., A.D. 1036 December 24. It refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya king Jagadēkamalladēva and records that Dēvaḷadēvi, the queen of Jagadēkamalladēva, and the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Udayāditya, “lord of Kāñchīpura” made a gift of land to Mahādēvashaṇaṅgi, Krishṇayya and Tuppada Dēvayya for having immersed the bones of Noḷambamahādēvī in the Ganges and for the service of a god (name lost).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . samastabhuvanāśrayaṁ śrīprithvīvallabhaṁ mahārā
jādhirāja paramēśvaraṁ paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāsrayakuḷa
tiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmajjagadēkamalladēvara vijayarājyamu
ttarōttarābhivṛddhipaprarddhamānamācaṁdrārkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire po
ṭṭaḷakeṟeya nelevīḍinalsukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyutta
mire svastyanavarata paramakalyāṇābhyudayasahasrapha
ḷabhōgabhāgini dvitīyalakṣmīsamāneyar ś®majjaga
dēkamalladēvara visāḷōrastaḷajayānganālabdhe noṇaṁbanaṇugi
nāmādisamastaprasastisahita śrimaddēvaḷadēviyaru samadhi
gatapañcamahāsabda mahāpallavānvaya śrīpṛthvīvallabhaṁ pa
llavakuḷatiḷakanamōghavākya kāṁcīpuravarēsvaraṁ śrīmanmahā
maṇḍaḷēśvara nodayādityadēvarumirddu sakavarṣa 958 neyaThe inscription is engraved in characters of a later period than the one to which it to belong.
dhātusaṁvatsaraduttarāyaṇasaṁkrāntiyaṁdu sṛmanno
ḷaṁbamahādēviyaraṁgamaṁ gaṁgeyoḷikki baṁda brāhmaṇa mādhava
seṇangiyarge kṟiṣṇayyaṁgaṁ tuppadadēvayyaṁgaṁ nalviḍi 12 ṟa kaṁ
paṇaṁ Orvāyaṁ kuḍe tṛḷōkapaṇḍitara siddayyaṁge śrī
rge nivēdyanandādīvigetrikālapūjegeṁdu dhārāpū
kariya nelammattaru 45 Ī dharma
The stone is broken here.
Digital edition of SII 9.92 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
